content.problemStatement:
Chef has a string and wants to cut it into substrings such that every substring is a palindrome. Chef wants to minimize the number of cuts needed to achieve this. Help Chef find the minimum number of cuts required so that each part of the partitioned string is a palindrome.

Chef needs your help to write a function that, given the input string, returns the smallest number of cuts needed to partition the string into palindromic substrings.

Function DeclarationFunction NamefindMinimumCutsfindMinimumCutsfindMinimumCuts - This function calculates the minimum number of cuts needed to partition a given text into palindromic substrings.

Parameters
inputStringinputStringinputString (string): The text to be partitioned into palindromic substrings. It consists of lowercase English letters only.
Return Value
Returns an integer integerResultintegerResultintegerResult representing the minimum cuts required.
The output is a non-negative integer indicating the smallest number of cuts needed.
Input Format
The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each of the next TTT lines contains a string inputStringinputStringinputString representing Chef's text.
Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing the minimum number of cuts required to partition the given string into palindromic substrings.
Constraints
1≤T≤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51≤T≤105
1≤∣inputString∣≤20001 \leq |inputString| \leq 20001≤∣inputString∣≤2000
Each inputStringinputStringinputString consists of lowercase English letters only.
constraints: S contains only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains a single integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a string S consisting of lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the minimum number of cuts required to partition the given string into palindromic substrings.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Each string is processed independently. 'a', 'racecar' and 'aabbbaa' are already palindromes, so 0 cuts. 'abcde' has no longer palindrome, requiring 4 cuts between each character.
'abba' and 'abcba' are palindromes → 0 cuts. For 'banana' the optimal split is 'b' | 'anana', giving 1 cut.

a → 0 cuts. abcde → each character separate → 4 cuts. The string 'abcde' needs 4 cuts. So output line is 4. Updated output: "0
4
1
2
0".

solution.approach: The problem can be solved using dynamic programming. First, we create a 2D table to store whether each substring is a palindrome or not. Then, we create another array to store the minimum number of cuts required for each substring. We fill up this array by iterating over the string and checking all possible substrings. If a substring is a palindrome, we update the minimum number of cuts required for that substring.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N^2) per test case, where N is the length of the string. The total time complexity is O(T * N^2) because we are iterating over the string for each test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N^2) — the dominant structure is the 2D table used to store whether each substring is a palindrome or not.
